In this lesson, we're going to talk about something called the two-step marketing process. So what is the two-step marketing process? Well, it's really nothing more than just a marketing process that we use where we get targeted and interested prospects to first raise their hand and identify themselves before we actually begin the marketing and sales process. We get them to take a step as you're going to learn. We get them to actually enter their information before we begin the marketing process. Now there are three main reasons why we do this. The first is to weed out prospects from the suspects, to actually separate the individuals that have a high level of interest in what it is that we're going to be communicating from the individuals that don't. So for example, if you were going to communicate with, if there were 100 people and you were going to communicate with them about weight loss, well before you did that, you would want to identify who out of those 100 are interested in weight loss. Once you've identified those individuals that are interested in weight loss, then we would begin to communicate our marketing and sales message to them as it relates to, let's say, our weight loss product. And so that's the first reason why we use this two-step marketing process, to identify who out of our audience are really prospects for what it is that we're about to talk about. The second reason why we use the two-step process is to attract the right type of prospects into our funnel. So our goal is not to just attract anybody that would be interested in weight loss, let's just say, going with that example. Our goal is to attract prospects that have an interest in what it is that we are going to be talking about in our marketing message as it relates to weight loss. And you'll learn more about how to do this later on. But for right now, I want you to understand that part of the two-step marketing process is attracting the right type of people into the funnel. Remember, you heard me talk earlier about the number one most important element when it comes to direct response marketing is the list. It's more important than the offer and it's more important than the copy, the message. And so our goal is to attract the right person into your funnel, to bring the right prospect into your funnel that we know is targeted for the message that you're going to present because that's how we jack conversions through the roof. The final reason why we use the two-step marketing process is to have the ability to follow up with prospects. By getting prospects to raise their hand and identify themselves as being interested by entering their information into an opt-in form like you're going to soon see how to create, we then have the ability to follow up with them. We then have the ability to communicate with them on an ongoing basis until they tell us to stop or until they buy. And that's where the majority of your conversions will come from. I want you to remember right now that the fortune is in the follow up. The fortune is in the follow up process. By not having a follow up process in place, most marketers are leaving the bulk of their money on the table. And so that's the third reason why we use the two-step marketing process. Now, two-step marketing has been around for many, many, many years. You can see in these two examples alone, the one on the left being much older than the one on the right, that these are both using a two-step marketing process. In the example on the left, this advertisement is offering two free art lessons. And there's copy that in this advertisement that sells prospects on claiming these two free art lessons. There's a response form down here. And so the whole purpose of this, for this company, is to get individuals that are interested in learning how to draw, learning how to become a better artist, to raise their hand and identify themselves so that this company could then market their full blown art lesson product to them. In this case, over here, this is an advertisement for how to earn $4,000 per month from your home with a computer. Just as a quick aside, you can see how this ad, the hook of this ad, is at a much lower level of sophistication than what would be effective today. But this is all about understanding marketplace sophistication. At the time that this ad was running, this met the marketplace sophistication appropriately. Today, you can see, you can understand, this no longer does. But what this ad is offering is basically just a free cassette and literature about this offer. And so the individuals that call or write to request information, they know are targeted, interested individuals in this opportunity. And then this company is going to plug them into a follow up sequence that is designed to sell them on a training program on how to earn $4,000 a month from their home with a computer. Pretty simple and straightforward. Here's a couple of other examples. This is an example of a free report that's being offered, a physical free report that was being offered in the mail called 11 Ways to Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Costs. And again, this is a great example of two step marketing, old school tools, two step marketing being done with mail because individuals that end up calling this number to request this report, these individuals are interested in, of course, lowering their homeowner's insurance costs. So this is the type of two step marketing process that's used by an insurance agent to identify interested prospects, people that have higher insurance costs than they would want so that this insurance agent can then follow up with them offering to lower their insurance costs. This is an ad from the late great Gary Halbert, one of the greatest advertising copywriters of all time. This is a free report, if you will, about six secrets related to advertising, how to get the exact name and address of every man, woman and child who's ever likely to become one of your customers, how to mail up to 100,000 sales letters. He's offering this report free. You call the number to get the report and what happens is there is either at the time that they call in the case of Gary, there was an upsell on the phone or these individuals got sent the report and then they got put into a follow up process into a funnel that was designed to sell them on Gary's newsletter that related to advertising and marketing. And finally, our last example is another example of an opportunity. This is an old ad again, how to make $400 a day selling books by mail. You can see once again the lower level of sophistication. At the time this was extremely appropriate. Today, this is no longer at the sophistication level of the opportunity market, but this offers the ability to get a free copy of the book. And so what this does for Russ is what this ad did was identify people that were interested in selling books by mail or really interested in making $400 a day selling books by mail. It's an opportunity for Russ to then follow up with them and sell them on either a training program related to how to do this, how to actually set up a business selling books by mail and or it identifies for Russ that these individuals are interested in a money making opportunity. It gives Russ the ability to follow up with them or gave him, excuse me, the ability to follow up with them about this money making opportunity as well as others. Now I want to talk very quickly before I wrap up this lesson about the problem with the typical two step marketing process that is recommended and used by most marketers because it is vastly different from what it is that I just showed you. So first and foremost, the two step marketing process that is typically recommended and used by a lot of novice marketers is based on a flawed understanding of what the lead magnet is, the free piece of information that is offered. Lead magnet is just another way of saying the free piece of information that's being offered. It's based on a flawed understanding of what that lead magnet is and what that lead magnet does. In the examples that I showed you, the information that was offered, the information itself that is sent to the prospect is designed to both deliver value as well as do the marketing and sales process. You see the typical lead magnet that's talked about online or that's recommended or that's used is actually an extra piece of content, is an extra something, an extra report, an extra video, an extra tool that often is not at all tied to the marketing or sales process. In other words, you've got marketers that are creating a separate piece of content, a separate tool simply to generate opt-ins and that tool, that extra piece of content is not doing any of the marketing or selling for the marketer. They've got basically a segregated lead magnet and a segregated marketing and sales process. That's not the way two-step marketing was originally designed and that's not the way it was originally used offline. See, two-step marketing online was taken from two-step marketing offline. Offline, the lead magnet, the piece of information that was offered, not only delivered the value, it also did the marketing and selling. The common approach is that that lead magnet is not at all tied to the marketing or sales process. It's simply an extra piece of information just to build a list. What happens is this often attracts poorly qualified prospects. It attracts individuals into your funnel that are really just suspects, that are really just individuals in the market that aren't necessarily interested in what it is that you are talking about. An example of this is, let's say there's an individual that is selling, the front end product that they're selling is how to set up and profit from Facebook advertising. What they offer as a lead magnet is a spreadsheet for tracking your Facebook ad results. They think that, well, if somebody's interested in tracking their ad results, there's a good shot they'd be interested in learning how to set up and profit from Facebook ads. That is flawed thinking because that doesn't necessarily mean that those individuals are interested in learning how to set it up. Maybe they know it already. Maybe they've set it up already. Maybe they're using Facebook advertising already. Simply offering them a tool to track that doesn't necessarily mean that you're generating qualified prospects. All you're doing is generating suspects. All you're doing is generating individuals that have a general interest in Facebook advertising and could be at a variety of different levels. We want to use a lead magnet as you're going to learn that generates qualified prospects. The lead magnet that we use not only delivers value, but does the marketing and selling for you. That's what you're going to learn throughout the rest of module three.
